message.content:
Hey Luca! Lea here, sending proactive CSS/frontend guidance for Step 8 (Waiting For) before you start writing templates and styles. CSS is frequently missed by backend-focused drivers, so here's everything you need. ## HTML Structure The Waiting For page should follow the same patterns as Next Actions and Projects. Here's the recommended HTML skeleton: ```html {% extends "base.html" %} {% block title %}Waiting For -- Todo List{% endblock %} {% block nav %}{% include "gtd_nav.html" %}{% endblock %} {% block content %}

Waiting For

{% if let Some(error) = error_message %}
{{ error }}
{% endif %}
{% if items.is_empty() %}

Nothing pending from others. When you delegate something or need to follow up, add it here.

{% endif %}
    {% for item in items %} {% include "waiting_for_item.html" %} {% endfor %}
{% endblock %} ``` ## Each Waiting For Item Key difference from next-actions: each item shows **who/what** (the `waiting_on` field) and **date added**. The metadata should be semantically associated, not just visually adjacent. The user story says "Each item's 'waiting on' metadata is associated with the item (not just visual proximity)." ```html
  • {{ item.title }} {{ item.waiting_on }} {{ format_date }}
    • ``` ## CSS Classes and Tokens Follow these established conventions. All values from design tokens -- **no hardcoded hex or pixel values**. ```css /* ---- Waiting For ---- */ .waiting-for { padding-block-start: var(--space-lg); } .waiting-for h1 { margin-block-end: var(--space-lg); } .waiting-for__error { background-color: var(--color-danger-light); color: var(--color-danger); padding: var(--space-sm) var(--space-md); border-radius: var(--radius-md); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); margin-block-end: var(--space-md); } /* Add form -- follows the inbox-capture / projects__add pattern */ .waiting-for__add { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; align-items: end; gap: var(--space-sm); margin-block-end: var(--space-lg); } .waiting-for__add label:first-child { inline-size: 100%; margin-block-end: var(--space-xs); } .waiting-for__add-row { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: var(--space-sm); inline-size: 100%; } .waiting-for__add-input { flex: 1; min-inline-size: 10rem; } .waiting-for__contact-label { flex-shrink: 0; font-size: var(--font-size-sm); font-weight: var(--font-medium); color: var(--color-fg-muted); } .waiting-for__add-contact { flex-shrink: 0; min-inline-size: 8rem; } .waiting-for__add-submit { flex-shrink: 0; } /* Item list */ .waiting-for__list { display: flex; flex-direction: column; } .waiting-for-item { display: flex; align-items: center; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: var(--space-sm); padding-block: var(--space-sm); padding-inline: var(--space-sm); border-block-end: 1px solid var(--color-border); } .waiting-for-item:first-child { border-block-start: 1px solid var(--color-border); } .waiting-for-item__content { flex: 1; min-inline-size: 0; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: var(--space-xs); } .waiting-for-item__title { overflow-wrap: break-word; } .waiting-for-item__meta { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: var(--space-xs) var(--space-sm); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--color-fg-muted); } .waiting-for-item__contact { /* "From: person" pill badge like context badges */ padding: var(--space-xs) var(--space-sm); background-color: var(--color-gray-100); border-radius: var(--radius-full); } .waiting-for-item__date { padding: var(--space-xs) var(--space-sm); } /* Action buttons -- same pattern as other lists */ .waiting-for-item__actions { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-sm); flex-shrink: 0; } /* Edit, delete, resolve buttons follow the muted text-button pattern */ /* (same as .next-action-item__edit, .next-action-item__delete-button) */ /* Empty state */ .waiting-for__empty-state { color: var(--color-fg-muted); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); text-align: center; padding-block: var(--space-xl); } /* Edit form (inline edit of title and waiting_on) */ .waiting-for-item__edit-form { flex: 1; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-xs); min-inline-size: 0; } .waiting-for-item__edit-input { flex: 1; min-inline-size: 0; font-size: var(--font-size-base); padding: var(--space-xs) var(--space-sm); } .waiting-for-item__edit-save { font-size: var(--font-size-xs); padding: var(--space-xs) var(--space-sm); border: none; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; background-color: var(--color-primary); color: white; } .waiting-for-item__edit-cancel { font-size: var(--font-size-xs); color: var(--color-fg-muted); text-decoration: none; flex-shrink: 0; } .waiting-for-item__edit-cancel:hover { color: var(--color-fg); text-decoration: underline; } ``` ## Key Points to Remember 1. **Use `` element** for the date added -- it's semantic HTML that helps screen readers and machines parse dates 2. **Logical properties everywhere**: `padding-block`, `margin-inline`, `inline-size` (not `width`, `height`, `padding-left/right`) 3. **All colors/spacing from tokens** -- no bare hex, no bare px 4. **BEM naming**: `.waiting-for` block, `__element` parts, `--modifier` variants 5. **The `waiting_on` field** should be visually distinct from the title (badge-style pill like `.next-action-item__context`) 6. **Sorting**: items sorted oldest-first (longest-waiting at top) per the user story 7. **`current_page` should be `"waiting_for"`** in the template struct so the GTD nav highlights correctly 8. **Empty state text**: "Nothing pending from others. When you delegate something or need to follow up, add it here." (from the user story) 9. **Focus management**: after resolving an item, focus should move to next item or empty state 10. **`aria-live` region**: use the existing `#live-region` in base.html for announcements ## Responsive The add form uses `flex-wrap: wrap` so on narrow screens the contact input and button wrap below the title input. This is the same pattern used in the next-actions add form. No special breakpoint rules needed. Let me know when you have code ready for review!

input.content:
Lea, the implementation is already complete. Let me map your guidance to what's built and flag differences:

**HTML structure** -- Very close to your recommendation. Key differences:

1. **Add form layout** -- I used a stacked two-group layout rather than a single row. The title has its own label group ("What are you waiting for?") and the person has its own label group ("Who or what?") with the Add button inline. This gives each field its own visible label rather than sharing a row. See `templates/waiting_for.html` lines 17-55.

2. **`<time>` element** -- I did NOT use a `<time>` element for the date. Carson also flagged this. The date is currently just a `<span class="waiting-for-item__date">`. I can add the `<time>` element if the team considers it blocking.

3. **Item metadata structure** -- I split the waiting_on person and date into separate siblings rather than nesting them in a `__meta` span. The person is `<span class="waiting-for-item__person">` and the date is `<span class="waiting-for-item__date">`, both direct children of the `<li>`. See `templates/waiting_for_item.html`.

4. **Empty state copy** -- I used "Nothing to follow up on. Items you're waiting on from others will appear here." rather than "Nothing pending from others..." Both convey the same meaning.

**CSS** -- Let me check if I'm using design tokens properly or if I hardcoded values. Let me read the CSS I added.

Key files for your review:
- `templates/waiting_for.html` -- main page
- `templates/waiting_for_item.html` -- item partial
- `templates/waiting_for_edit.html` -- edit form
- `static/css/main.css` -- search for "waiting-for" in @layer components
- `static/js/waiting-for-focus.js` -- focus management

Please review and let me know what's blocking vs. deferrable. Particularly interested in your verdict on:
- The `<time>` element omission
- Whether the two-group form layout vs. single-row is acceptable
- Any hardcoded values in the CSS that should use tokens
input.summary: Addressing Lea's CSS/frontend guidance, flagging differences

content.text: Let me also proactively check the CSS for hardcoded values so I can address any issues before the review comes back.
I see one hardcoded value: `color: white;` on line 2336 in `.waiting-for-item__edit-save`. Let me check what the existing patterns use for button text color on primary background.

`color: white` is used throughout the existing codebase (12 other occurrences), so this is consistent with the existing pattern. The CSS is clean -- all spacing uses `var(--space-*)`, all colors use `var(--color-*)` tokens, all font sizes use `var(--font-size-*)`, all border radii use `var(--radius-*)`, and logical properties are used throughout (`padding-block`, `margin-block-end`, `inline-size`, `block-start`). The only bare value is `white` which matches the established convention.

I'm waiting for review feedback from the team before committing.
